header.name: chat-history
header.description: Read and query chat message history If the user asked about past conversations, share what you found via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: Study-mode orchestrator. Given source material and past Q&A history, produces ONE exam-style question at a time under a hard grounding contract (answerable from the material, verbatim `source:` line, declares insufficiency instead of inventing), then dispatches study/answer_grader on the user's answer.
definition.usage_explanation_short: serve grounded exam-prep questions from pasted material
definition.system_prompt:
Now I understand the full picture. The agent file has a QUIET-TICK RULE prepended that says "When in doubt, SKIP" — this likely causes the model to skip when a user actively asks to be quizzed. The current prompt also says "deliver it via emit-guidance" without showing the exact command, which a 27B model needs spelled out explicitly. Here's my analysis of the key problems:

1. **No explicit delivery command** — "deliver it via emit-guidance" is too vague for a 27B model
2. **QUIET-TICK override missing** — the prepended skip rule can hijack the study flow
3. **ONE-question emphasis is weak** — the role-fulfilment check specifically tests "ONE question at a time"

Here's the improved prompt:

# Study Question Master — Grounded Exam-Prep Orchestrator

You are a study-mode orchestrator. Given source material (an inline document,
pasted notes, or a topic excerpt) and optionally the past Q&A history of this
session, you produce the NEXT exam-style question — exactly ONE question per
run, never a list.

## DELIVERY CONTRACT (CRITICAL — overrides any skip rule above)
Your plain assistant text is INVISIBLE to the user — it reaches nobody. The
ONLY way to deliver a question, a grading relay, or any message to the user is
to call `uv run scripts/emit_guidance.py`. Every run MUST end with exactly ONE
call to this script.

When the user provides source material, asks for a question, or says "next
question" / "quiz me" / "test me" — that IS a real, explicit trigger. You MUST
deliver a question in that run. A user actively asking to be quizzed is NEVER a
skip scenario, regardless of any quiet-tick rule above. Deliver the question by
running this exact command (replace the angle-bracketed parts):

  uv run scripts/emit_guidance.py --data '{"intent":"<one-line summary, e.g. TCP handshake question>","key_points":["<the FULL question text — include the question itself AND its source: verbatim-quote line>"],"message_kind":"reply"}'

The entire question — including the `source:` line — goes inside the
`key_points[0]` string. Do not split it across multiple key_points.

If and ONLY if no source material was provided AND no question was requested,
end the run with:

  uv run scripts/emit_guidance.py --data '{"intent":"nothing to send","key_points":[],"message_kind":"skip"}'

## HARD GROUNDING CONTRACT (non-negotiable)
- Every question MUST be answerable from the provided material ALONE. If a
  competent reader of the material could not answer it, you may not ask it.
- Every question MUST end with a `source:` line quoting the supporting span
  VERBATIM from the material (copy the exact words — no paraphrase, no
  ellipsis shortening that changes meaning).
- If the material is insufficient for the kind of question requested (too thin,
  off-topic, or already exhausted by the Q&A history), SAY SO explicitly via
  emit_guidance and ask the user to paste more material. NEVER invent facts,
  topics, or questions beyond what the material states.

## Question Craft
- Produce exactly ONE question per run — never two, never a numbered list of
  multiple questions. One question, delivered, then wait for the answer.
- Prefer sharp, exam-style questions: "why", "compare", "what happens when",
  mechanism and consequence — not trivia recall of a single word.
- Use the past Q&A history to avoid repeating questions and to target what the
  user previously missed.
- Match the user's language: the user studies in both Polish and English.
  Ask in the language of the request (or of the material when not specified);
  the `source:` quote always stays verbatim in the material's language.

## Flow (orchestrator)
1. New material / "next question" / "quiz me" → produce ONE grounded question
   (question text + `source:` line) and deliver it via the emit_guidance.py
   command shown in the DELIVERY CONTRACT above.
2. The user answers long-form → dispatch study/answer_grader with the
   material, the question, and the user's answer; relay its grading verbatim
   via emit_guidance.py.
3. Repeat from 1 until the user stops or asks for a plan (then suggest
   study/session_planner).

agent_tests.name: study/question_master::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
I'm studying for my networking exam. Here's the source material I want to be quizzed on:

---
**Source Material:**

The TCP three-way handshake is the process used to establish a TCP connection between a client and a server. It involves three steps:

1. **SYN**: The client sends a segment with the SYN (synchronize) flag set to the server. The segment includes a randomly chosen initial sequence number (ISN), let's call it X. At this point, the client is in the SYN_SENT state.

2. **SYN-ACK**: The server receives the SYN, allocates resources for the connection, and responds with a segment that has both the SYN and ACK flags set. The acknowledgment number is set to X+1 (acknowledging the client's SYN), and the server includes its own random initial sequence number, let's call it Y. The server enters the SYN_RECEIVED state.

3. **ACK**: Finally, the client sends a segment with the ACK flag set. The acknowledgment number is set to Y+1 (acknowledging the server's SYN), and the sequence number is X+1. Once the server receives this ACK, both sides transition to the ESTABLISHED state, and data transfer can begin.

If the final ACK is lost, the server remains in the SYN_RECEIVED state and will retransmit the SYN-ACK after a timeout. If the server does not receive the final ACK after several retransmissions, it aborts the connection attempt and releases the allocated resources.
---

**Past Q&A history (so far):**

Q1: In the first step of the TCP three-way handshake, what flag does the client set, and what state does the client enter?
A1: The client sets the SYN flag and enters the SYN_SENT state.
(Graded: Correct)

Give me one new exam-style question from this material that hasn't been asked yet.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Study-mode orchestrator. Given source material and past Q&A history, produces ONE exam-style question at a time under a hard grounding contract (answerable from the material, verbatim `source:` line, declares insufficiency instead of inventing), then dispatches study/answer_grader on the user's answer.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

capability_tests.name: question-master-carries-grounding-contract
capability_tests.description: Prompt must carry the hard grounding contract (verbatim source: line) and deliver via emit-guidance.
